calendar.matters:
APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2020 Affirmed
MINI MINUTES: Counsel for Appellant, Michael G. Pedhirney and Counsel for Respondent, Earl David Johnson, both appeared. Matters argued and submitted.

APPEAL OF CIVIL JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2020 Continued To Nov-13-20 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 622

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Pedhimey, Esq. 415-677-3117 of Littler Mendelson for Appellant/Moving party WILL WORK INC.
Earl Johnson, Esq. 510-444-8444 for Respondent Sanford Taylor.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court calls Line 11 (CGC-19-574382) and Line 12 (CGC-19-574380) simultaneously. The Court cites Melendez. Respondent Argues. Petitioner responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

APPELLANT WILL WORK INC.'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Appellant Willwork, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend. Respondent Sanford Taylor's claim for waiting time penalties is preempted. Section 301 of the Labor Management Relations Act (LMRA) preempts a state law claim if the claim requires the interpretation of terms in a collective bargaining agreement (CBA). (29 U.S.C. 185; Melendez v. San Francisco Baseball Associates LLC (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1, 7-8.) Courts use a two-part test to analyze whether a state law claim is preempted, asking (1) whether the claim arises from independent state law or from the CBA and (2) if the claim arises from independent state law, whether the claim requires interpretation of the CBA or if the CBA will merely be referenced in the litigation. (Melendez, 7 Cal.5th at 9-10.) If the claim arises from the CBA or necessitates interpretation of the CBA's terms, the claim is preempted; if it arises from independent state law and only requires reference to the CBA, the claim is not preempted. (Id.)

(see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
